sections.paragraph: (for חֵלְכָא) quadril. adj. (with the addition at the end of א and ה, see Lehrg. p. 865), m. Psa 10:8, in pause חֵלֶ֑כָה ver. 14 Psa 10:14, pl. חֵלְבָאִים ver. 10 Psa 10:10 כתיב, the wretched, the unfortunate, as rightly rendered in the ancient versions. Others render חלבה “thy host (O God),” and חל באים (which also the Masora directs to be written as two words) “the host of the afflicted;” but the interpretation previously given is preferable.
